evidence_excerpt: The lesion resolved completely after discontinuation of 6-mercaptopurine and two cycles of cyclophosphamide, and she remains disease-free at 14 months; there was no recurrence after 18 months of follow-up.

quality_of_life.functional_status_evidence_excerpt: After complete regression of the eyelid lesion and sustained disease-free status (14–18 months), no functional limitations were described.
quality_of_life.symptom_burden_evidence_excerpt: Lesion regressed completely with no recurrence reported up to 18 months, suggesting absence of ongoing symptoms.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Following two cycles of cyclophosphamide, the lesion regressed completely.
She remains disease-free at 14 months following diagnosis, and the lesion has not recurred after 18 months of follow-up.

notes_on_uncertainty: Functional status and quality-of-life details were not explicitly documented; inferred from complete lesion resolution and sustained disease-free status. There is a minor discrepancy in long-term follow-up durations reported (14 vs 18 months), both indicating no recurrence.
